MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-18-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE ERRONEOUS DISMISSAL. DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL IS GRANTED CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFF'S NON-OPPOSITION TO A REQUEST FOR A NEW TRIAL DATE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A NEW TRIAL DATE SHOULD BE DIRECTED TO DEPARTMENT 206. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
ORDER GRANTING EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND RELATED DEADLINES

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Erroneous Dismissal
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE ERRONEOUS DISMISSAL

Appearances:
Mark Iodice of Morgan and Morgan (949.898.6502, mark.iodice@forthepeople.com) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and moving party;
Pamela Stevens of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4291, pamela.stevens@sfcityatty.org) for defendant and responding party.

Discussed. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to vacate dismissal is granted conditioned on plaintiff's non-opposition to a request for a new trial date. Defendant's request for a new trial date should be directed to Department 206. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
